articles.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
articles.subtitle: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suit
articles.author: Hayden Field
articles.summary: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for "high-volume work"; and Luna, a […]
body: The new release includes three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for “high-volume work”; and Luna, a “fast and affordable” everyday model. OpenAI says it’s especially skilled at coding, cybersecurity, and biology, as well as staying focused during long-horizon agentic AI tasks.

Per million tokens, GPT-5.6 Sol is priced at $5 input / $30 output (nearly half the cost of Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, which is $10 input / $50 output). Terra is half the cost of Sol, and Luna is less than half the cost of Terra. The company also debuted two additional modes for Sol: a “max” mode for deeper reasoning and an “ultra” mode for leveraging sub-agents — evoking OpenClaw, and perhaps a sign of OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger ’s work at OpenAI so far.

Unsurprisingly amid a security panic in Washington, D.C. , OpenAI dedicated the majority of its announcement blog post to safety and potential misuse. It appeared to reference the recent jailbreaking travails of its rival Anthropic, writing that “GPT‐5.6 is trained to refuse prohibited cyber assistance, including when users attempt to disguise their intent or jailbreak the model.” It also said that flagship model Sol “is better at helping people find and fix vulnerabilities than reliably carrying out end-to-end attacks,” and that Sol doesn’t cross the cyber-critical threshold under OpenAI’s preparedness framework — though it should be noted that OpenAI recently revised its preparedness framework in April and removed some areas of previous study.

The company said Sol has the company’s “most robust safety stack to date” and that it “strengthened protections for higher-risk activity, sensitive cyber requests, and repeated misuse.” OpenAI said it had dedicated “approximately 700,000 A100e GPU hours” to automated red-teaming and also worked with third-party testers, the latter of which will continue to test it for the next two weeks.

OpenAI also seemed to be taking an extra-sensitive approach during the preview period, which is being closely monitored by the Trump administration. The company wrote that “safeguards may occasionally intervene on legitimate work, particularly in dual-use areas where defensive and offensive activity can initially look similar. That is part of what the preview is designed to test.” The report earlier this week said that the Trump administration will approve customers on a case-by-case basis during the preview period.

OpenAI said the model suite should be generally available in the coming weeks because the company believes in “broad access,” and that the company cooperated with the US government ahead of this launch, but that it hopefully wouldn’t be the norm.

“We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” the company wrote. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them. We are taking this short-term step because we believe it is the strongest path to broader availability in the coming weeks, while we work with the Administration to develop the cyber Executive Order framework and a repeatable process for future model releases.”
keyPoints: The new release includes three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for “high-volume work”; and Luna, a “fast and affordable” everyday model. OpenAI says it’s especially skilled at coding, cybersecurity, and biology, as well as staying focused during long-horizon agentic AI tasks.

Per million tokens, GPT-5.6 Sol is priced at $5 input / $30 output (nearly half the cost of Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, which is $10 input / $50 output). Terra is half the cost of Sol, and Luna is less than half the cost of Terra. The company also debuted two additional modes for Sol: a “max” mode for deeper reasoning and an “ultra” mode for leveraging sub-agents — evoking OpenClaw, and perhaps a sign of OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger ’s work at OpenAI so far.
paragraphTranslations: 新版本包括三个模型：Sol、Terra和Luna。
在新闻爆出OpenAI将应特朗普政府的请求分阶段发布下一个模型后不到24小时，那个模型GPT-5.6就来了。周五，公司推出了其新GPT 5.6模型套件的有限预览：Sol，旗舰型号；Terra，用于“大量工作”的中端模型；以及Luna，一个“快速且经济”的日常模型。OpenAI表示，它特别擅长编码、网络安全和生物学，以及在长期地平线代理AI任务中保持专注。

每百万个标记，GPT-5.6 Sol的定价为输入5美元/输出30美元（几乎是Anthropic的Claude Fable 5的一半，后者为输入10美元/输出50美元）。Terra的价格是Sol的一半，而Luna的价格不到Terra的一半。公司还为Sol推出了两种额外模式：一种“max”模式用于更深入的推理，以及一种“ultra”模式用于利用子代理——这让人联想到OpenClaw，也许是OpenClaw创始人Peter Steinberger在OpenAI迄今为止工作的体现。

在华盛顿特区的安全恐慌中，OpenAI将其公告博客文章的大部分内容都献给了安全和潜在的滥用。它似乎提到了其竞争对手Anthropic最近的越狱困境，写道“GPT-5.6被训练为拒绝禁止的网络协助，包括当用户试图掩饰他们的意图或越狱模型时。”它还表示，旗舰模型Sol“在帮助人们发现和修复漏洞方面比可靠地执行端到端攻击更胜一筹”，并且Sol在OpenAI的准备框架下没有越过网络关键阈值——尽管值得注意的是，OpenAI在4月份最近修订了其准备框架，并删除了一些先前研究的领域。
公司表示，Sol拥有公司迄今为止“最强大的安全栈”，并且它“加强了对高风险活动、敏感网络请求和重复滥用的保护。”OpenAI表示，它已经投入了“大约700,000个A100e GPU小时”到自动化红队测试中，并且与第三方测试人员合作，后者将继续在未来两周内对其进行测试。
OpenAI似乎在预览期间采取了额外敏感的方法，这正在被特朗普政府密切监控。公司写道，“安全措施可能会偶尔介入合法工作，特别是在防御和攻击活动最初看起来相似的双重用途领域。这是预览旨在测试的一部分。”本周早些时候的报告称，特朗普政府将在预览期间逐案批准客户。
OpenAI表示，由于公司相信“广泛获取”，模型套件应该在接下来的几周内普遍可用，并且公司在这次发布前与美国政府合作，但希望这不会成为常态。
“我们不相信这种政府访问流程应该成为长期默认设置，”公司写道。“它使最好的工具无法被用户、开发者、企业、网络防御者和全球合作伙伴所使用。我们采取这一短期步骤，是因为我们相信这是未来几周更广泛可用性最强的路径，同时我们与政府合作开发网络行政命令框架和未来模型发布的可重复流程。”

paragraphs: OpenAI has released GPT-5.6, a new AI model suite, which includes three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol is the main model, Terra is for high-volume work, and Luna is for everyday use. The models are skilled in coding, cybersecurity, and biology. GPT-5.6 is priced lower than a competitor's model, Claude Fable 5. OpenAI has focused on safety and preventing misuse, especially in cybersecurity. The company has worked with the US government and is testing the models for safety. They hope to make the models widely available soon.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI发布了GPT-5.6，这是一个包含三个模型的新AI模型套件：Sol、Terra和Luna。Sol是主要模型，Terra用于大量工作，Luna用于日常使用。这些模型在编码、网络安全和生物学方面技能娴熟。GPT-5.6的定价低于竞争对手的模型Claude Fable 5。OpenAI专注于安全性和防止滥用，尤其是在网络安全方面。该公司与美国政府合作，正在测试模型的安全性。他们希望很快使这些模型广泛可用。

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
articles.subtitle: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.
articles.author: BBC World
articles.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.
body: Israel and Lebanon have signed a framework agreement in Washington after several days of negotiations brokered by the US.
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the agreement will begin to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security.
It comes as limited fighting has continued between Israeli forces and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, despite an existing ceasefire.
Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and it is unclear whether it will agree to withdraw its fighters from the South Litani area in southern Lebanon.
Washington has feared that ongoing tension between Israel and Hezbollah could undermine its peace deal with Iran, which includes a commitment to end fighting on "all fronts", including Lebanon.
In the 14-point framework agreement, Israel and Lebanon both "affirm" the right of each state to "live in peace", and express "mutual desire to live in security as neighboring sovereign states".

It makes specific note to a "cessation of all hostile or adverse actions in international political or legal fora" between Israel and Lebanon, with both nations pledging to work towards the release of detainees, as well as the return of any remains.
However, both governments acknowledge that "nothing in this Framework prevents them from exercising their inherent right to defend themselves".

The agreement says that the Lebanese Armed Forces (LAF) will restore effective sovereign authority over all Lebanese territory, "pending the verified disarmament of non-state armed groups and dismantlement of associated infrastructure".
In order to achieve this, Lebanon makes a specific request for the support of international and "particularly Arab partners, under the leadership of the US".
A US-supported military coordination group will also be established to help implement the framework.
From a diplomatic perspective, the signing of some kind of an agreement is a step forward, but the situation on the ground in Lebanon has shown little sign of shifting, despite several ceasefires.
keyPoints: Israel and Lebanon have signed a framework agreement in Washington after several days of negotiations brokered by the US.
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the agreement will begin to put in place a framework for lasting peace and security.
It comes as limited fighting has continued between Israeli forces and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, despite an existing ceasefire.
paragraphTranslations: 以色列和黎巴嫩在美国斡旋下经过几天的谈判后，在华盛顿签署了框架协议。
美国国务卿马尔科·鲁比奥表示，该协议将开始建立持久和平与安全的框架。
尽管存在停火协议，以色列军队和伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党之间的有限战斗仍在继续。
真主党不是周五协议的一方，目前尚不清楚它是否会同意从黎巴嫩南部利塔尼河以南地区撤出其战斗人员。
华盛顿担心，以色列和真主党之间持续的紧张关系可能会破坏其与伊朗的和平协议，该协议包括承诺在包括黎巴嫩在内的“所有战线”结束战斗。
在14点框架协议中，以色列和黎巴嫩都“确认”每个国家有权“和平生活”，并表达了“作为邻国主权国家生活在安全中的共同愿望”。
它特别提到以色列和黎巴嫩之间在国际政治或法律论坛上“停止所有敌对或不利行动”，两国都承诺努力释放被拘留者以及归还任何遗体。
然而，两国政府都承认“该框架中的任何内容都不妨碍他们行使自卫的固有权利”。
该协议指出，黎巴嫩武装部队（LAF）将在所有黎巴嫩领土上恢复有效的主权权威，“在非国家武装团体经过核实的解除武装和拆除相关基础设施之前”。
为了实现这一点，黎巴嫩特别请求国际和“特别是阿拉伯伙伴，在美國的领导下”的支持。
还将建立一个美国支持的军事协调小组，以帮助实施该框架。
从外交角度来看，某种协议的签署是一个进步，但黎巴嫩当地的局势尽管有几项停火协议，却显示出很少的转变迹象。

paragraphs: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement in Washington after US-brokered talks. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the agreement will start building a framework for lasting peace and security. Despite a ceasefire, limited fighting continues between Israeli forces and Iranian-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah is not part of the agreement, and it's unclear if they will withdraw from the South Litani area. The agreement includes a 14-point framework where both countries affirm the right to live in peace and express a mutual desire for security. Both governments also acknowledge their right to defend themselves. Lebanon requested international support, especially from Arab partners led by the US, to restore sovereign authority over its territory.
paragraphTranslations: 以色列和黎巴嫩在华盛顿签署了一项框架协议，该协议是在美方斡旋后达成的。美国国务卿马尔科·鲁比奥表示，该协议将开始建立持久和平与安全的框架。尽管存在停火协议，以色列军队和伊朗支持的真主党在黎巴嫩南部的有限战斗仍在继续。真主党不是该协议的一部分，目前尚不清楚他们是否会从南部利塔尼地区撤军。该协议包括一个14点框架，两国都确认了和平生活的权利，并表达了相互安全的愿望。两国政府还承认他们有权自卫。黎巴嫩请求国际支持，特别是由美国领导的阿拉伯伙伴，以恢复对其领土的主权。
